rulings.Case Title: ERIC FOSTER VS. BAE SYSTEMS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Eric Foster'S Motion To Augment Expert Witness Designation
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 5, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ERIC FOSTER'S Motion To Augment Expert Witness Designation. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: (1) The motion is granted as to augmentation of the expert witness designations with all parties having leave to augment their expert witness designations. The Court finds good cause exists to permit augmentation by plaintiff and that defendants will not suffer substantial prejudice as a result. Similarly, good cause further exists to permit augmentation by defendants in response to the designation of Professor Johnson. The parties are to exchange augmented designations within seven (7) days and to complete expert discovery no less than fifteen (15) days prior to the new trial date. The Court notes that the order assigning the discovery referee does not bar the Court from hearing motions pertaining to the designation of experts, and the present motion does not involve the type of complex fact based issues best determined by the referee. Any issue as to the admissibility of Professor Johnson's testimony is the province of the trial court and not a proper basis for refusing an augmented designation. (2) The request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SEIFER, MURKEN, DESPINA, JAMES & TEICHMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer To Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration/Complaint In Quantum Meruit And To Enforce Lien
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 5, 2013, Line 3: RESPONDENT ALAN HYAMS' Hearing On Demurrer To Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration/Complaint In Quantum Meruit And To Enforce Lien Overruled. Weiss v. Marcus rejected the contention that Fracasse v. Brent ((1972) 6 Cal.3d 784) abrogated the rule that an attorney who has been discharged is entitled to enforce his lien to the extent of the reasonable value of his services prior to being discharged. ((1975) 51 Cal.App.3d 590.) Weiss held that a discharged attorney may recover the quantum meruit value of his services by enforcing a lien established in a written agreement. (Id.) Cal. Code of Civ. Proc. § 337 provides that "an action upon any contract, obligation, or liability founded upon an instrument in writing¿" has a statute of limitation of four years. The liability alleged is founded upon an instrument in writing as plaintiff seeks to enforce the lien provision in the written contingency fee agreement. (Amended Petition/Complaint, 6, "Petitioner, therefore, amends this pleading to proceed in this Court action against Respondent to recover on its underlying claim, to wit, an action to enforce its lien and to recover in quantum meruit for the value of the services it rendered and does hereby assert those claims.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: HENRY WOLF VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bmw Of North America, Llc'S Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiff'S Responses To Discovery And Awarding Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 5, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANT BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC'S Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiff's Responses To Discovery And Awarding Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel responses is granted in part and denied in part. If substantive responses have not been provided prior to the hearing, substantive responses to the discovery must be served on moving party within seven (7) days of the entry of the order. Moving party's request for sanctions is denied as to present counsel or plaintiff. The Court finds that extraordinary circumstances exist (the recent disbarment of plaintiff's former counsel) that would make it unjust to impose sanctions against the plaintiff or present counsel. rulings.Case Title: HENRY WOLF VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Redacted Versions Submitted Pending Motion To Seal)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 5, 2013, Line 17: DEFENDANT CORBIN-PACIFIC, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Redacted Versions Submitted Pending Motion To Seal) The Court grants defendant Corbin-Pacific's motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff fails to establish a design defect in the motorcycle seat or causation. Defendant maintained its initial burden of production by relying on the declarations of Mr. Yeldham, Mr. Corbin, and Dr. Kessler. In opposition, plaintiff fails to present competent evidence demonstrating a triable issue of material fact regarding design defect or causation. The Court denies plaintiff's request for a continuance under CCP 437c(h). Plaintiff fails to demonstrate diligence and the trial date is December 16, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: HENRY WOLF VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 5, 2013, Line 17: DEFENDANT BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication The Court grants defendant BMW's motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff fails to establish a design defect in the motorcycle or causation. Defendant maintained its initial burden of production by citing plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses and also by relying on the declarations of Mr. Yeldham and Dr. Kessler. In opposition, plaintiff fails to present competent evidence demonstrating a triable issue of material fact regarding design defect or causation. The Court denies plaintiff's request for a continuance under CCP 437c(h). Plaintiff fails to demonstrate diligence and the trial date is December 16, 2013. rulings.Case Title: MARTIN LEUNG VS. CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTH CARE ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder And Or Non-Oppos To Chinese Community Hearlth Care Associations Mtn To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 5, 2013, Line 11: DEFENDANT CHINESE COMMUNITY HEALTH PLAN'S Joinder And Or Non-Opposition To Chinese Community Health Care Association's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings Granted. Plaintiff entered into an agreement with Defendant Chinese Community Health Care Association ("CCHCA") that governs the terms of his compensation. (Declaration of Edward A. Chow, Ex. 1.) The arbitration provision in the agreement is enforceable by Defendant CCHCA as a signatory to the agreement. The arbitration agreement is enforceable by Defendant Chinese Community Health Plan, a non-signatory to the agreement that joins in Defendant CCHCA's motion, through the doctrine of equitable estoppel. The claims pled in the complaint relate to Plaintiff's compensation, which is governed by the agreement. Therefore, Plaintiff's claims are intertwined with the agreement and equitable estoppel applies. (See Molecular Analytical Systems v. Ciphergen Biosystems, Inc. (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 696, 706.) Pursuant to Cal. Code. Civ. Proc. § 1281.4, the entire suit is stayed pending the outcome of arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ON BEHALF OF VS. STATE OF NEVADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Specially-Appearing Defts To Quash Service Of Summons For Improper Service And Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 5, 2013, Line 13: Specially-Appearing Defendants STATE OF NEVADA, NEVADA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES [DHHS], MICHAEL J. WILLDEN, IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS DIRECTOR OF DHHS, NEVADA DIVISION OF MENTAL HEALTH & DEVELOPMENTAL SERVICES [MHDS], RICHARD WHITLEY, IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS ADMINISTRATOR OF MHDS, SOUTHERN NEVADA ADULT MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES [SNAMHS], RAWSON-NEAL PSYCHIATRIC HOSPITAL, A NEVADA STATE MENTAL HEALTH FACILITY OPERATED BY SNAMHS, CHELSEA SZKLANY, IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS HOSPITAL ADMINISTRATOR FOR SNAMHS AND RAWSON-NEAL PSYCHIATRIC HOSPITAL'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Improper Service And Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction The motion is off calendar. The case was assigned to Judge Munter in the complex litigation department for all purposes. rulings.Case Title: ACCESS PUBLIC RELATIONS LLC VS. DEEM INC. (FKA REARDEN COMMERCE INC.)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 5, 2013, Line 14: DEFENDANT DEEM INC.'S (FKA REARDEN COMMERCE INC.) MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT The Court grants the motion to strike with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead a statutory or contractual basis for the fee request. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ACCESS PUBLIC RELATIONS LLC VS. DEEM INC. (FKA REARDEN COMMERCE INC.)
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 5, 2013, Line 14: DEFENDANT DEEM INC.'S (FKA REARDEN COMMERCE INC.) DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads the breach of contract cause of action according to the contract's legal intendment and effect. Paragraph 8 of the complaint pleads the terms of the contract, paragraph 9 pleads plaintiff's performance, and paragraphs 7 and 11 allege that the contract is written. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM
